title: Oncidium gilvum
intro: Oncidium gilvum, also known as Yellow Oncidium, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Oncidium gilvum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oncidium gilvum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Oncidium gilvum is classified within the genus Oncidium, which belongs to the subtribe Oncidiinae and tribe Cymbidieae in the family Orchidaceae. This classification places Oncidium gilvum among the orchid family. The Orchidaceae family is a large and diverse group, with over 30,000 known species worldwide. Oncidium gilvum is specifically a type of epiphytic orchid, which grows on other plants or surfaces for support. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Oncidium gilvum and its related species.
